title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn Reed meets with Walter Fane to discuss making a new will, as her previous relatives in New Zealand have died. During the meeting, Gwendolyn attempts to probe Fane's past by asking if he knew her mother, Meg Harley. Fane denies knowing Meg, explaining that he was once engaged to Helen Kennedy, who eventually married Colonel Harrydyke. Fane reveals that Helen traveled to India to marry him but changed her mind and met Gwendolyn's father on her return journey. Fane notes that Gwendolyn's father and Helen lived in Dalhousie for a time. Fane recalls carrying Gwendolyn on his shoulders when she was a small child and mentions that he left India after an unsuccessful attempt to grow tea to join his family's legal firm. He invites Gwendolyn and her husband to meet his mother for tea and schedules a follow-up meeting for Thursday at eleven o'clock.

Later, Gwendolyn discusses Fane with Jules by the seaside. She argues that Fane is too quiet, gentle, and upright to be a murderer, though Jules suggests that quiet individuals like Liz Burton or Wallace can also be suspects. Despite her hesitation, Gwendolyn recalls Fane's strange, unfocused gaze when Saint Catherine's Villa was mentioned.

In a separate scene, Gwendolyn visits Mrs. Montford's drawing room and meets Edith Peggitt, the sister of Mrs. Montford. Edith reminisces about Gwendolyn's childhood, recalling how Gwendolyn used to mispronounce "raisins" as "pouf-gan" when visiting her. Edith also mentions a former nanny named Léonie.
